base: Build a simple 3D platformer. The game is rendered in 3D with a perspective camera (not a 2D canvas). The player controls a 3D character that moves left and right with the A and D keys and jumps with the Space key. The level contains at least three 3D platforms at varying heights and a goal object (e.g. a flag or marker) on one of them. The player wins by reaching the goal. The player loses if they fall off the bottom of the level. Display a win/lose screen when the game ends.

perScenario.opencode-plain: Using Babylon.js 8 (already loaded from CDN in index.html), write the entire game in game.js. Use a BABYLON.Scene with a perspective camera (ArcRotateCamera or UniversalCamera), proper 3D lighting, and 3D meshes (BABYLON.MeshBuilder.CreateBox / CreateSphere / etc.). Do NOT use the 2D canvas API or draw shapes with 2D context — the result must be rendered by Babylon's 3D engine. Do not edit index.html.
perScenario.opencode-roblox: Using Roblox Studio via the provided MCP tools, build the game in a blank baseplate place. Place all code in ServerScriptService or StarterPlayerScripts as appropriate.
